steps.title: Read the first graph's key values
steps.content: The first graph: parabola opens downward, starts at y = 7 when x = 0, vertex near (0.3, 8.3).

steps.title: Apply the platform change
steps.content: The second platform is 2 feet shorter: 7 - 2 = 5 ft. The launch mechanism is identical, so the parabolic trajectory shifts straight down by 2 feet. Every y-value decreases by 2.

steps.title: Compute new key points
steps.content: Starting height: 7 - 2 = 5. Vertex: (0.3, 8.3 - 2) = (0.3, 6.3). Height at x ≈ 0.6: 7 - 2 = 5.

steps.title: Evaluate all four choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘• The parabola opens downward. • The vertex is at the approximate point (0.3 comma 5.0). • The parabola passes through the following points: • approximately (0 comma 3.7) • approximately (0.3 comma 5.0) • approximately (0.6 comma 3.7)’: Starting height 3.7 would mean a 3.7-ft platform (7 - 3.3), not 5 ft. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘• The parabola opens downward. • The vertex is at the approximate point (0.3 comma 6.3). • The parabola passes through the following points: • (0 comma 5) • approximately (0.3 comma 6.3) • approximately (0.6 comma 5)’: Starting height 5 ft matches 7 - 2 = 5, and vertex 6.3 matches 8.3 - 2 = 6.3. ✓
  • Choice C — ‘• The parabola opens downward. • The vertex is at the approximate point (0.3 comma 10.3). • The parabola passes through the following points: • (0 comma 9) • approximately (0.3 comma 10.3) • approximately (0.6 comma 9)’: Starting height 9 would mean a 9-ft platform, which is 2 feet taller, not shorter. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘• The parabola opens downward. • The vertex is at the approximate point (0.3 comma 15.3). • The parabola passes through the following points: • (0 comma 14) • approximately (0.3 comma 15.3) • approximately (0.6 comma 14)’: Starting height 14 is far above 5; does not match any reasonable shift. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
